metadata.summary: Pandas and Polar DataFrames as interactive DataTables

documentation:
![ITables logo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mwouts/itables/3f8e8bd75af7ad38a500518fcb4fbbc370ea6c4c/itables/logo/wide.svg)

[![CI](https://github.com/mwouts/itables/actions/workflows/continuous-integration.yml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/mwouts/itables/actions)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/mwouts/itables/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/mwouts/itables?branch=main)
[![Pypi](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/itables.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/itables)
[![Conda Version](https://img.shields.io/conda/vn/conda-forge/itables.svg)](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/itables)
[![pyversions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/itables.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/itables)
[![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black)
[![Jupyter Widget](https://img.shields.io/badge/Jupyter-Widget-F37626.svg?style=flat&logo=Jupyter)](https://mwouts.github.io/itables/widget.html)
[![Streamlit App](https://static.streamlit.io/badges/streamlit_badge_black_red.svg)](https://itables.streamlit.app)

This packages changes how Pandas and Polars DataFrames are rendered in Jupyter Notebooks.
With `itables` you can display your tables as interactive [DataTables](https://datatables.net/)
that you can sort, paginate, scroll or filter.

ITables is just about how tables are displayed. You can turn it on and off in just two lines,
with no other impact on your data workflow.

The `itables` package only depends on `numpy`, `pandas` and `IPython`
which you must already have if you work with Pandas in Jupyter (add `polars`, `pyarrow` if you
work with Polars DataFrames).

## Documentation

Browse the [documentation](https://mwouts.github.io/itables/) to see
examples of Pandas or Polars DataFrames rendered as interactive DataTables.

## Quick start

Install the `itables` package with either
```shell
pip install itables
```

or
```shell
conda install itables -c conda-forge
```

Activate the interactive mode for all series and dataframes with
```python
from itables import init_notebook_mode

init_notebook_mode(all_interactive=True)
```
and then render any DataFrame as an interactive table that you can sort, search and explore:
![df](docs/df_example.png)

If you prefer to render only selected DataFrames as interactive tables, use `itables.show` to show just one Series or DataFrame as an interactive table:
![show](docs/show_df.png)

Since ITables v1.0, the [jQuery](https://jquery.com/) and [DataTables](https://datatables.net/) libraries and CSS
are injected in the notebook when you execute `init_notebook_mode` with its default argument `connected=False`.
Thanks to this the interactive tables will work even without a connection to the internet.

If you prefer to load the libraries dynamically (and keep the notebook lighter), use `connected=True` when you
execute `init_notebook_mode`.

## Supported environments

ITables works in all the usual Jupyter Notebook environments, including Jupyter Notebook, Jupyter Lab, Jupyter nbconvert (i.e. the tables are still interactive in the HTML export of a notebook), Jupyter Book, Google Colab and Kaggle.

You can also use ITables in [Quarto](https://mwouts.github.io/itables/quarto.html) HTML documents, and in RISE presentations.

ITables works well in VS Code, both in Jupyter Notebooks and in interactive Python sessions.

Last but not least, ITables is also available as
- a [Jupyter Widget](https://mwouts.github.io/itables/widget.html)
- a [Streamlit](https://mwouts.github.io/itables/streamlit.html) component,
- and it also works in [Shiny](https://mwouts.github.io/itables/shiny.html) applications.

functions.name: itables.utils.find_package_file
documentation: Return the full path to a file from the itables package

functions.name: itables.utils.read_package_file
documentation: Return the content of a file from the itables package

functions.name: itables.downsample.downsample
documentation: Return a subset of the dataframe that fits the limits

functions.name: itables.downsample._downsample
documentation: Implementation of downsample - may be called recursively

modules.name: itables.interactive
documentation: Activate the representation of Pandas dataframes as interactive tables

modules.name: itables.version
documentation: ITables' version number

methods.name: itables.widget.ITable.update
documentation:
Update either the table data, attributes, or the arguments passed
to DataTable. Arguments that are not mentioned

functions.name: itables.sample_dfs.get_countries
documentation:
A Pandas DataFrame with the world countries (from the world bank data)
Flags are loaded from https://flagpedia.net/

functions.name: itables.sample_dfs.get_population
documentation: A Pandas Series with the world population (from the world bank data)

functions.name: itables.sample_dfs.get_indicators
documentation: A Pandas DataFrame with a subset of the world bank indicators

functions.name: itables.sample_dfs.get_pandas_styler
documentation:
This function returns a Pandas Styler object

Cf. https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/user_guide/style.html

modules.name: itables.options
documentation:
Global options for ITables.

These parameters are documented at
https://mwouts.github.io/itables/advanced_parameters.html

variables.name: itables.options.layout
documentation:
Show the index? Possible values: True, False and 'auto'. In mode 'auto', the index is not shown
if it has no name and its content is range(N)

variables.name: itables.options.showIndex
documentation: Default DataTables classes. See https://datatables.net/manual/styling/classes

variables.name: itables.options.classes
documentation:
Default table style. Use
- 'table-layout:auto' to compute the layout automatically
- 'width:auto' to fit the table width to its content
- 'margin:auto' to center the table.
Combine multiple options using ';'.

NB: When scrollX=true, "margin:auto" is replaced with "margin:0"
to avoid an issue with misaligned headers

variables.name: itables.options.style
documentation: Additional tags like e.g. caption

variables.name: itables.options.tags
documentation: Maximum bytes before downsampling a table

variables.name: itables.options.maxBytes
documentation: Maximum number of rows or columns before downsampling a table

variables.name: itables.options.maxColumns
documentation: By default we don't sort the table

variables.name: itables.options.order
documentation: Pre dt code

variables.name: itables.options.pre_dt_code
documentation: Optional table footer

variables.name: itables.options.footer
documentation: Column filters

variables.name: itables.options.column_filters
documentation: Should a warning appear when we have to encode an unexpected type?

variables.name: itables.options.warn_on_unexpected_types
documentation: Should a warning appear when the deprecated 'dom' is used?

variables.name: itables.options.warn_on_dom
documentation:
Should a warning appear when the selection targets rows that have been
filtered by the downsampling?

variables.name: itables.options.warn_on_selected_rows_not_rendered
documentation: The DataTables URL for the connected mode

variables.name: itables.options.dt_url
documentation: The DataTable bundle for the offline mode

variables.name: itables.options.dt_bundle
documentation: Display the ITables animated logo when loading

functions.name: itables.shiny.init_itables
documentation:
Load the DataTables library and the corresponding css (if connected=False),
and (if all_interactive=True), activate the DataTables representation for all the Pandas DataFrames and Series.

Warning: make sure you keep the output of this cell when 'connected=False',
otherwise the interactive tables will stop working.

functions.name: itables.shiny.DT
documentation: This is a version of 'to_html_datatable' that works in Shiny applications.

modules.name: itables.javascript
documentation: HTML/js representation of Pandas dataframes

functions.name: itables.javascript.init_notebook_mode
documentation:
Load the DataTables library and the corresponding css (if connected=False),
and (if all_interactive=True), activate the DataTables representation for all the Pandas DataFrames and Series.

Warning: make sure you keep the output of this cell when 'connected=False',
otherwise the interactive tables will stop working.

functions.name: itables.javascript._table_header
documentation: This function returns the HTML table header. Rows are not included.

functions.name: itables.javascript._flat_header
documentation: When column filters are shown, we need to remove any column multiindex

functions.name: itables.javascript.json_dumps
documentation:
This is a replacement for json.dumps that
does not quote strings that start with 'function', so that
these functions are evaluated in the HTML code.

functions.name: itables.javascript.replace_value
documentation:
Set the given pattern to the desired value in the template,
after making sure that the pattern is found exactly once.

functions.name: itables.javascript.to_html_datatable
documentation:
Return the HTML representation of the given
dataframe as an interactive datatable

functions.name: itables.javascript.get_itables_extension_arguments
documentation:
This function returns two dictionaries that are JSON
serializable and can be passed to the itables extensions.
The first dict contains the arguments to be passed to the
DataTable constructor, while the second one contains other
parameters to be used outside of the constructor.

functions.name: itables.javascript.check_table_id
documentation:
Make sure that the table_id is a valid HTML id.

See also https://stackoverflow.com/questions/70579/html-valid-id-attribute-values

functions.name: itables.javascript.to_html_datatable_using_to_html
documentation:
Return the HTML representation of the given dataframe as an interactive datatable,
using df.to_html() rather than the underlying dataframe data.

functions.name: itables.javascript._df_fits_in_one_page
documentation: Display just the table (not the search box, etc...) if the rows fit on one 'page'

functions.name: itables.javascript.show
documentation: Show a dataframe

classes.name: itables.javascript.JavascriptFunction
documentation: A class that explicitly states that a string is a Javascript function

classes.name: itables.javascript.JavascriptCode
documentation: A class that explicitly states that a string is a Javascript code

functions.name: itables.datatables_format._isetitem
documentation: Older versions of Pandas don't have df.isetitem

functions.name: itables.datatables_format.datatables_rows
documentation: Format the values in the table and return the data, row by row, as requested by DataTables
